User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile within the Code Ascent application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The user's chosen username, unique across the platform.

lastLogin.description: The date and time of the user's last login.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the user account was created.

Language.title: Language
Language.description: Defines a programming language supported by the Code Ascent application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Language entity.

name.description: The full name of the programming language (e.g., 'Python', 'JavaScript').

description.description: A brief description of the programming language.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing the programming language.

Topic.title: Topic
Topic.description: Represents a broad programming topic or category within a specific language.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Topic entity.

name.description: The name of the topic (e.g., 'Variables', 'Control Flow', 'Object-Oriented Programming').

description.description: A detailed description of the topic.

languageId.description: Reference to the Language this topic belongs to. (Relationship: Language 1:N Topic)

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific, granular programming skill or concept, serving as a node in the visual skill mastery tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Declare Variables', 'Define Functions', 'Implement Linked List').

description.description: A detailed description of what mastering this skill entails.

topicId.description: Reference to the Topic this skill belongs to. (Relationship: Topic 1:N Skill)

prerequisiteSkillIds.description: References to other Skills that must be mastered before attempting this skill. (Relationship: Skill N:N Skill)

difficultyLevel.description: The inherent difficulty level of the skill (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents an individual educational content unit, typically comprising explanations and interactive exercises.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

description.description: A brief summary of what the lesson covers.

contentMarkdown.description: The lesson's educational content, formatted in Markdown.

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill this lesson helps to teach. (Relationship: Skill 1:N Lesson)

order.description: The sequential order of this lesson within its parent skill.

estimatedCompletionMinutes.description: An estimated time in minutes required to complete the lesson.

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: Represents an interactive coding task or problem embedded within a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exercise entity.

description.description: The problem statement or prompt for the coding exercise.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this exercise is part of. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Exercise)

initialCode.description: Boilerplate code provided to the user to start the exercise.

solutionCode.description: The correct solution code for the exercise (for internal reference or hints).

testCases.description: An array of objects defining test cases to validate user solutions. Each object contains input and expected output/behavior.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the exercise (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a user's progress and completion status for individual lessons.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is progressing through the lesson. (Relationship: User 1:N UserLessonProgress)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson being tracked. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserLessonProgress)

status.description: The current progress status of the lesson for the user (e.g., 'not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed').

completedAt.description: The date and time when the user completed the lesson, if applicable.

lastAccessedAt.description: The date and time when the user last accessed this lesson.

UserExerciseAttempt.title: UserExerciseAttempt
UserExerciseAttempt.description: Records each attempt a user makes on an exercise, including their submitted code, AI feedback, and execution results.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExerciseAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the attempt. (Relationship: User 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

exerciseId.description: Reference to the Exercise that was attempted. (Relationship: Exercise 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

submittedCode.description: The code submitted by the user for this attempt.

submissionTime.description: The date and time when the code was submitted.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the submitted code passed all test cases.

aiFeedback.description: Personalized feedback generated by the client-side AI on the submitted code.

executionOutput.description: The raw output generated by executing the user's submitted code.

errorDetails.description: Details of any compilation or runtime errors encountered during execution.

UserSkillMastery.title: UserSkillMastery
UserSkillMastery.description: Stores a user's mastery level for a specific skill, used for dynamic learning path adaptation and the visual skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSkillMastery entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose skill mastery is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserSkillMastery)

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill whose mastery level is being tracked. (Relationship: Skill 1:N UserSkillMastery)

masteryLevel.description: A numerical score (e.g., 0-100) indicating the user's mastery level for the skill.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the mastery level was last updated.

identifiedWeakness.description: Indicates whether this skill has been identified as a weakness for the user by the AI.

strengthScore.description: An AI-derived score representing the user's strength in this skill.

weaknessScore.description: An AI-derived score representing the user's weakness in this skill.

AIChallenge.title: AIChallenge
AIChallenge.description: Represents a dynamically generated, personalized coding challenge created by the client-side AI to target specific user weaknesses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChallenge entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User for whom this challenge was generated. (Relationship: User 1:N AIChallenge)

targetSkillId.description: Reference to the Skill that this challenge is designed to improve. (Relationship: Skill 1:N AIChallenge)

prompt.description: The problem statement or description for the AI-generated challenge.

initialCode.description: Boilerplate code provided to the user to start the challenge.

testCases.description: An array of objects defining test cases to validate user solutions for this challenge.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the AI-generated challenge (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

generationDate.description: The date and time when this challenge was generated by the AI.

completionDate.description: The date and time when the user completed this challenge, if applicable.

status.description: The current status of the AI challenge for the user (e.g., 'pending', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'skipped').

associatedAttemptId.description: Reference to the UserExerciseAttempt associated with the user's solution to this challenge, if any. (Relationship: UserExerciseAttempt 1:1 AIChallenge)

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each document's ID is the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the user themselves for write operations, with potentially public read access for basic profile information like username.
Root collection for all supported programming languages. This data is publicly readable and serves as static content.

params.name: languageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a programming language.

definition.description: Subcollection storing topics within a specific language. This data is publicly readable and represents static content.

params.name: languageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent language.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier for a programming topic.

definition.description: Subcollection storing granular programming skills within a specific topic and language. This data is publicly readable and forms the basis of the skill mastery tree.

params.name: languageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent language.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent topic.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific skill.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual educational content units (lessons) tied to a specific skill. This data is publicly readable.

params.name: languageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent language.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent topic.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent skill.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific lesson.

definition.description: Subcollection storing interactive coding exercises embedded within specific lessons. This data is publicly readable.

params.name: languageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent language.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent topic.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent skill.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific exercise.

definition.description: Tracks a user's progress and completion status for individual lessons. This is user-specific data, only accessible by the 'userId' specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (matching request.auth.uid).

params.name: userLessonProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's lesson progress record.

definition.description: Records each attempt a user makes on an exercise. This is user-specific data, only accessible by the 'userId' specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (matching request.auth.uid).

params.name: userExerciseAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's exercise attempt record.

definition.description: Stores a user's mastery level for a specific skill. This is user-specific data, only accessible by the 'userId' specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (matching request.auth.uid).

params.name: userSkillMasteryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user's skill mastery record.

definition.description: Represents a dynamically generated, personalized coding challenge for a user. This is user-specific data, only accessible by the 'userId' specified in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (matching request.auth.uid).

params.name: aiChallengeId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AI-generated challenge.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Code Ascent prioritizes Authorization Independence and adherence to QAPs (Queries as Permissions) by segregating static, public educational content from dynamic, user-specific progress data. Educational content such as `Languages`, `Topics`, `Skills`, `Lessons`, and `Exercises` are structured hierarchically as subcollections. For instance, `Lessons` are nested under `Skills`, which are under `Topics`, themselves under `Languages`. This hierarchy simplifies content discovery and management, and since all content is publicly readable, it avoids complex security rules.

User-specific data, including `UserLessonProgress`, `UserExerciseAttempt`, `UserSkillMastery`, and `AIChallenge`, is secured using Path-Based Ownership. Each of these user-specific collections is a subcollection directly under a specific user's document, e.g., `/users/{userId}/lessonProgress/{userLessonProgressId}`. This design is critical for **Authorization Independence** because it embeds the `userId` directly into the document path. Consequently, security rules can enforce ownership by simply comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents or external lookups. This makes rules robust, atomic, and easy to debug.

This structure also effectively supports **QAPs** (Queries as Permissions). For the public content (Languages, Topics, Skills, Lessons, Exercises), all read operations (including `list`) are inherently secure as they are intended to be globally accessible. For user-specific data, a `list` query on a path like `/users/myUserId/lessonProgress` is implicitly secure, as the rules will only permit access if `myUserId` matches `request.auth.uid`. A user cannot query `/users/otherUserId/lessonProgress` because the security rules, based on the path, would deny access, thus preventing unauthorized data leakage through `list` operations.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by clearly separating global content from user-specific data. All documents within a given collection or subcollection share the same security posture (e.g., all documents in `/languages/{languageId}/topics/{topicId}/skills/{skillId}/lessons/{lessonId}/exercises` are public read, while all documents in `/users/{userId}/exerciseAttempts` are private to that user). This greatly simplifies the security rule logic. **Data Clarity and Predictability** are maintained through descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{languageId}`, `{skillId}`) and explicit `status` fields in progress and challenge entities, rather than relying on inferred states.

Regarding the user's specific request to 'correct all lessons', the current hierarchical structure for `Lessons` (nested under `Skill`, `Topic`, `Language`) is a robust and scalable approach for organizing educational content. It naturally groups lessons within their context and aligns with the app's learning path structure, making them easily retrievable by skill or topic. No fundamental correction is needed for this structure, as it already supports logical organization and public accessibility of lessons.
